name: Ashwagandha Extract
historicalUse: Ashwagandha is a foundational rasayana herb in Ayurvedic medicine, documented in Charaka Samhita as a premier adaptogen for strength, vitality, and cognitive resilience. Ibn Sina referenced Withania (under the Arabic name ubab) in the Canon for its strengthening and sleep-promoting properties. It has been in continuous medical use for over 3,000 years across South Asian and Middle Eastern traditions.
modernEvidence: KSM-66® ashwagandha (5% withanolides, full-spectrum root extract) is the most clinically validated ashwagandha form with 24+ clinical trials. Evidence supports significant cortisol reduction, improvement in stress and anxiety scores (GAD-7), testosterone support in men, improvement in VO2 max, and neuroprotection. Withanolides modulate HPA axis reactivity and have GABA-mimetic anxiolytic properties.
mechanisms: HPA axis modulation — reduces cortisol hypersecretion, normalizes stress response
GABA-mimetic activity — withanolides bind GABA-A receptors, promoting anxiolysis
Thyroid stimulation — supports T3/T4 synthesis (caution in hyperthyroid states)
Testosterone support — reduces SHBG and LH suppression via cortisol reduction
Neuroprotection — withanolides inhibit beta-amyloid aggregation and tau phosphorylation
benefits: Supports healthy stress response and cortisol balance
Promotes cognitive calm and mental resilience
Supports physical vitality and exercise recovery
safetyProfile: Excellent safety profile with KSM-66®. Rare reports of GI discomfort. Drowsiness possible — best taken in evening if sedation is a concern.
contraindications: Hyperthyroidism or Graves' disease — ashwagandha stimulates thyroid hormone synthesis
Autoimmune conditions — theoretical immune-stimulating activity; caution in MS, lupus, rheumatoid arthritis
Pregnancy — may have uterotonic properties; avoid in pregnancy
drugInteractions: Thyroid medications — additive thyroid-stimulating effect; monitor TSH
Immunosuppressants — theoretical immune stimulation may oppose immunosuppression
Sedatives and benzodiazepines — additive CNS sedation possible
Diabetes medications — additive glucose-lowering effect; monitor blood glucose
physicianNotes: Avoid in hyperthyroid patients. Monitor TSH in patients on thyroid medications. Exercise caution in autoimmune disease patients on immunosuppression. KSM-66® is the preferred form — full-spectrum, milk-processed per traditional method, with the strongest clinical evidence base. Highly appropriate in HPA axis dysregulation, stress-related cognitive decline, and athletic performance support.
patientGuidance: Can be taken morning or evening. Taking it in the evening may support sleep quality. If you have thyroid disease or take thyroid medication, consult your physician before use.
